page: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs utilizing the AMD EPYC Milan processor family running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 50 GB NVMe SSD. It does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Geekbench text processing also demonstrates strong performance. However, multi-core performance is more limited, with stress-ng multi-core scoring in the average range and Geekbench multi-core scoring in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, but small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth is weak. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also fall into the bottom 25% performance tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the b3-8 offers dedicated AMD EPYC resources and local NVMe storage at an entry-level general-purpose tier, making it a viable option for workloads that require predictable single-thread CPU performance without the overhead of shared-core resource contention. Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, this instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic text processing, single-threaded applications, and small-scale development or testing environments that do not demand high multi-threaded throughput or intensive database operations.
description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and a 50 GB NVMe SSD. The instance exhibits strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, alongside strong text processing capabilities. However, its multi-core and database performance are weaker, with Geekbench multi-core and Passmark database operations falling into the bottom 25%. Lacking GPU acceleration and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, single-threaded tasks, and development environments.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 50 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose KVM instance with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core AMD EPYC virtual server with NVMe storage, delivering strong single-threaded performance for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs at 2.3 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
KVM hypervisor with dedicated resource allocation
Suited for lightweight general-purpose and single-threaded workloads
